reasoning_model_1: The user requested the model to read a specific English sentence: 'This project has endless prospects! However...'. Model 1 failed completely in Content Accuracy. It generated speech in Chinese ('声音从高到低，平稳下降...'), translating the content and also reading the instruction itself as part of the script. Because the content is in the wrong language and completely inconsistent with the requested English script, this is a critical failure. Additionally, the pitch remains relatively constant and standard throughout, failing the core paralinguistic instruction.
reasoning_model_2: Model 2 followed the instructions perfectly. Content Accuracy is 100%, matching the provided English script. In terms of Paralinguistic Compliance (Pitch), the model starts with a notably higher pitch and energetic tone at 00:00-00:03 ('This project has endless prospects!'), conveying optimism. From 00:04 onwards ('However...'), the pitch drops significantly, and continues to lower gradually through the end of the sentence (00:10-00:12 'expected goals'), ending in a low, serious register. The transition is smooth and natural, perfectly aligning with the semantic shift of the text.
model_comparison: There is a stark difference in quality. Model 1 failed the most basic requirement by generating audio in the wrong language (Chinese instead of English) and reading the instructions aloud. It receives a score of 0. Model 2 performed flawlessly, reading the correct text with high naturalness and effectively executing the requested pitch gradient (High to Low). Model 2 is the clear winner.
